content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

May 12, 1998


Vigo G. Nielsen, Jr.
Nielsen, Merksamer, Parrinello,
Mueller & Naylor, LLP
591 Redwood Highway
Mill Valley, CA 94941

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. G-98-123

Dear Mr. Nielsen:

You have requested advice concerning the campaign disclosure provisions of the Political
Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Specifically, you have requested clarification of the Commission's
advice that candidates and committees may file "estimated" late contribution reports pursuant to
Section 84206 covering the entire late contribution reporting period and must amend those
reports if the actual amount of contributions differs substantially from the estimate.

This is to inform you that staff will present a proposed regulation addressing this issue to
the Commission at its August meeting.

If you have any questions concerning this letter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By: Carla Wardlow
Chief, Technical Assistance Division

_________________________

¹ Government Code sections 81000-91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109-
18995, of the California Code of Regulations.

sections.question_synthetic: Whether candidates and committees may file estimated late contribution reports under Section 84206 and must amend those reports if actual contributions differ substantially from estimates.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC staff will present a proposed regulation addressing the issue of estimated late contribution reports to the Commission at its August meeting, but does not provide a definitive answer in this letter.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains no formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION/SHORT ANSWER sections. The actual legal question is embedded in the body text. This appears to be a procedural response deferring the substantive answer to a future regulatory proposal rather than providing direct advice. The document_type matches the provided input.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether candidates and committees may file estimated late contribution reports under Section 84206 and must amend those reports if actual contributions differ substantially from estimates.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC staff will present a proposed regulation addressing the issue of estimated late contribution reports to the Commission at its August meeting, but does not provide a definitive answer in this letter.

embedding.summary: The FPPC responds to an attorney's request for clarification regarding estimated late contribution reporting under Section 84206 of the Political Reform Act, indicating that staff will present a proposed regulation to address this issue at the August Commission meeting.
